arts.ttl: Cullen's double leads Wales to Nations League promotion with Iceland win
arts.sum: Wales won Nations League promotion as Liam Cullen’s first two international goals helped them to a 4-1 victory over Iceland.

arts.ttl: Germany denied by last-chance penalty for Hungary in Nations League. Sweden's Gyökeres scores 4
arts.sum: Dominik Szoboszlai scored a penalty in the ninth minute of stoppage time to deny Germany a victorious end to the Nations League group stage and show why Hungary is one of the hardest places to win in world soccer. After a video review, Szoboszlai stepped up to take the penalty and chipped it over goalkeeper Alexander Nübel to salvage a 1-1 draw for Hungary, which ends Group A3 third of four teams.

arts.ttl: Wales seal Nations League promotion after Liam Cullen sparks rout of Iceland
arts.athr: Ben Fisher at Cardiff City Stadium
arts.sum: Given the strides Wales have made under Craig Bellamy in the past three months, for supporters the only dampener is that they now have to wait another four to witness the next step on this journey. The bonus is that this result means Wales can look towards making a head start in their bid to qualify for another World Cup. Harry Wilson, who has now scored four goals in his past five matches for his country, capped an impressive comeback victory over Iceland, who led inside seven minutes courtesy of Andri Gudjohnsen. Liam Cullen scored twice before Brennan Johnson got on the scoresheet and Wilson thrashed in a fine strike from distance. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Wales 4-1 Iceland: Liam Cullen at the double as thumping win hands hosts Nations League promotion
arts.sum: Wales are into the League A of the Nations League after thumping Iceland at the Cardiff City Stadium

arts.ttl: Liam Cullen leads Wales to Nations League promotion
arts.sum: Topping their group also gives the Dragons a 2026 World Cup qualification boost.

arts.ttl: Wales level against Iceland - Latest score
arts.sum: Keep up to date with all the action from the Cardiff City Stadium as Wales host Iceland looking to seal promotion to League A of the UEFA Nations League.

arts.ttl: Wales v Iceland: Nations League – live
arts.sum: Nations League updates from the 7.45pm GMT kick-off Live scoreboard | Read Football Daily | And email Scott Craig Bellamy speaks to S4C. “The players know the art of defending the box extremely well … we have to keep that tonight … defending deep in the box we are exceptional at … freshness is very important … we’re completely aware of the data … some hit a certain level and it wouldn’t be the wisest decision to play them again today … we always want to do well but I want the players to be healthy … in good positions when they go back to their clubs … I’m looking forward to tonight … a really good game the way the group lies … it will definitely be an exciting game … the danger is everywhere … set plays they’re extremely dangerous … forwards very good … experience everywhere else … a good group of young players … I’m very fond of them … it’s definitely going to be a very difficult game tonight.” Ten years ago, Gareth Bale scored this goal in a friendly against Iceland … Continue reading...
